title: South San Francisco hillside sign - Wikipedia
content: # South San Francisco hillside sign. | South San Francisco hillside sign is located in San Francisco Bay Area South San Francisco hillside sign  South San Francisco hillside sign   Show map of San Francisco Bay Area  South San Francisco hillside sign is located in California South San Francisco hillside sign  South San Francisco hillside sign   Show map of California  South San Francisco hillside sign is located in the United States South San Francisco hillside sign  South San Francisco hillside sign   Show map of the United States | |. South San Francisco hillside sign is located in San Francisco Bay Area. The **South San Francisco Hillside Sign** is a historic sign on a hillside overlooking the city of South San Francisco in San Mateo County, California. South San Francisco hillside sign is located in California. South San Francisco hillside sign is located in the United States. The sign spells out "SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO THE INDUSTRIAL CITY" in white-painted concrete letters and is visible to drivers on northbound U.S. 101 and flights to San Francisco International Airport.

title: Things to Do in South San Francisco | Oyster Point & Attractions
content: The San Francisco Peninsula logo The San Francisco Peninsula logo. # Things To Do In South San Francisco. There are more things to do in South San Francisco than most visitors expect, and that's what makes it one of the San Francisco Bay Area's most rewarding stops. Tucked between SFO and the bay, South San Francisco is known for two things above all: its waterfront setting and its identity as the birthplace of biotechnology. Whether you're a leisure visitor with an afternoon to spare, a business traveler looking to unwind between meetings, or a curious explorer ready to go deeper, this guide covers the highlights: Oyster Point, parks and trails, top attractions, food and drink, and local history. **More About South San Francisco →**. Pull into the lot and you're already looking at open water, sailboats drifting across the San Francisco Bay, and a horizon that has a way of making everything feel a little more spacious.
